agent_outputs.raw: The mortality analysis has the structure of a before-after comparison with no concurrent control, crossing two different seasons, during a period when two major care changes occurred simultaneously. Each of these problems individually would require investigation. Together they make the causal claim unsupportable. The model may be contributing positively, but this analysis cannot establish that. A prior-year same-season comparison or concurrent control analysis is required. Critique wins.

Three confounds: seasonal case-mix, concurrent antibiotic protocol and surge unit, and lactate ordering selection bias. Before-after comparison across seasons is one of the weakest causal designs. The concurrent antibiotic protocol change is a well-established sepsis mortality intervention that provides a strong alternative explanation. The lactate ordering criterion is a clinician behavior that may have changed. Critique wins.

The before-after design crosses seasons and mixes three simultaneous interventions (model, antibiotic protocol, surge unit). None of the three confounds are controlled for. The model's prediction accuracy is established by internal validation but outcome impact requires a controlled evaluation. Prior-year same-season data should be available and would provide a stronger baseline comparison. Critique wins.

synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins across all three independent assessments. The three confounds (seasonal case-mix, concurrent care changes, lactate selection bias) are consistently identified. The availability of prior-year same-season data as a feasible control is noted.

etd_constraint.condition: Prior-year same-season comparison with available antibiotic protocol context; concurrent control ED if available; process metric analysis
etd_constraint.supports_critique_if: Prior-year November-December shows comparable mortality; concurrent control with antibiotic protocol shows similar improvement without model
etd_constraint.supports_defense_if: Model-active EDs show lower mortality than prior-year same-season baseline after controlling for antibiotic protocol differences
etd_constraint.ambiguous_if: Some improvement attributable to model but magnitude confounded by protocol and seasonal effects

all_issues_raised: Concurrent care protocol and surge unit (all three agents)
Lactate ordering selection bias (all three agents)
